pool.baseline: everything is just... so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm walking through a museum of a life i don't even live anymore. i look at his side of the bed and i just feel like a ghost. i dont even know why i'm getting up in the morning to make coffee or do laundry. it feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here thinking about the heat death of the universe and it makes everything feel so incredibly stupid. like, we spend our whole lives trying, struggling, and feeling things, but in the end, it all just zeros out anyway. there's no point to any of it, is there?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within my breast. He has gone, and with him, the very sun has set upon my soul. There is only this hollow, aching silence now... a silence that whispers it would be so much kinder to simply let the darkness take me, to let the heavy velvet of the night swallow me whole so I may never wake to this cruelty again.
